Theological manuscript, composite manuscript. Origin: Klosterneuburg Augustiner-Chorherrenstift. Description: https://manuscripta.at/hs_detail.php?ID=757. The f. 139b to 144b contain historiae, antiphonae, responsoria, lectiones cum neumis de sancto Thomas, ordo paschalis.

Composite manuscript. The piece is notation as a 13th-century addition on the very last folio. Origin: Garsten abbey (Austria). Description: https://digi.landesbibliothek.at/viewer/fullscreen/324/172/, https://manuscripta.at/hs_detail.php?ID=33976
Described in Konrad Schiffmann, Die Handschriften der öffentl. Studienbibliothek in Linz, p. 182 (https://digi.landesbibliothek.at/viewer/resolver?urn=urn:nbn:at:AT-OOeLB-1547261)

This ms. fragment of 8 fol. contains the so-called "Wiener Passionsspiel" or "Ludus paschalis latino-germanicus notis modulisque musicis instructus". Concordances with CB have to be checked. Origin: Niederalemannien. Notation: lotharingian neumes cf. Rhenull neumes, f and c clefs, b flat. Catalogues: https://handschriftencensus.de/3644, https://search.onb.ac.at/permalink/f/sb7jht/ONB_alma21295918700003338

13th cent, from Villers-la-Ville (cistercian abbey). Ad cor tuum revertere (CB 26) à la fin du manuscrit avant deux arbres représentants les  vices et les vertus.

HSB, vol. I.3, p. 131: 13th century, second half, from Toscany. Destroyed by fire in 1940, only reproductions available in Gragger, R. Eine altungarische Marienklage, Berlin-Leipzig 1923.

library: Musée Provincial des Arts anciens du Namurois

Diurnale-rituale. Provenance: Albi cathedral, chapter library. Notation: diastematic neumes, Aquitanian, c clef, custos

JOHANNES DAMASCENUS (Ps.), Vita ss. Barlaam et Josaphat, trad. anon. ; Translatio sancti Jacobi apostoli ; BEDA ; etc. Provenance: a cistercian abbey (https://initiale.irht.cnrs.fr/codex/1470) Reproduction IRHT Arca (scan from microfilm).

Manuscript destroyed in 1944. Pictures of fragments on the IRHT website, but nothing survives from the fol. with CB 119 except a photography taken by Yves Delaporte (1927) and held at the Bibliothèque diocésaine of Chartres / Solesmes. Composite manuscript, add. on f. 66v dated c. 1300. Provenance: Chartres cathedral, chapter library. Notation: Breton neumes, four-line staff, f clef. Some West Frankish neumes mixed with the Breton forms (clivis). Description of the ms. in the CGM: https://ccfr.bnf.fr/portailccfr/jsp/index_view_direct_anonymous.jsp?record=eadcgm:EADC:D1701128, online catalogue of the Chartres manuscripts: https://www.manuscrits-de-chartres.fr//fr/manuscrits/chartres-bm-ms-223
12th cent. Ms. with Isidore's Etymologies. Partial digitisation only

Composite manuscript, contains Sermons; patristic and classical excerpts; proverbs; computational table; Latin songs. Notation: West-Frankish neumes on red four-line staves, c, g, clefs. Biblio: Brewer, Charles Everett. 2013. Two Fragmentary Latin Song Collections from Thirteenth-Century Normandy. Cantus Planus: Papers read at the 15th meeting [I.M.S. Study Group on Chant] Dobogókő, Hungary 2009, I:315-38. Ottawa: The Institute for Mediæval Music. Jeudy, Colette, and Yves-François Riou (editors). 1989. Manuscrits classiques latins des bibliothèques publiques de France.  Paris: CNRS. Pages: vol. 1: 622-628. The description of the ms. in DIAMM is incomplete and partly wrong: https://www.diamm.ac.uk/sources/3868/#/ There is actually no polyphony on fols. 2v to 6. The Inventory is incomplete: https://www.diamm.ac.uk/sources/3868/#/inventory. According to DIAMM: Origin: England. Owner: Lyre abbey. Copied by several hands; material is arranged with distinct layouts and inconsistent quire numbering. May be connected to English dependencies of Lyre Abbey (notes of English names and a colophon recorded travel to England in 1246) CGM indicates the presence of "hymnes, avec musique notée" (f. 2v-6) - Dic qui gaudes prosperis et veste purpurea - Ego mundi timens naufragium - Ave, virgo Maria, Dei plena gratia - Planctus ante nescia - Licet eger cum egrotis - Est tonus sic, ut, re, ut, aut re, mi, re

Notation: square notation, still close to the "petits-carrés liés" type, red four-line staves, c, g clefs. A bit later than F-EV lat. 2, but maybe also of English origin? Composite manuscript, according to IRHT/Arca (https://arca.irht.cnrs.fr/ark:/63955/md70zp38wn2x):"Hymni, cum neumis ; PHILIPPUS CANCELLARIUS, Sermones abbreviati ; GREGORIUS ; AUGUSTINUS ; etc."CGM description: https://ccfr.bnf.fr/portailccfr/jsp/index_view_direct_anonymous.jsp?record=eadcgm:EADC:D08140238
Graduale of Alienor of Brittany. Provenance and use: Notre-Dame of Fontevraud, Fontevraud abbey. Notation: square notation, four red lines, c and F clefs, b flat

Seen 30.03.24, photos taken in person. Miscellanea (devotion, spirituality). Provenance: Notre Dame of Clairvaux (cistercian). Origin: France, East. A few reproductions here: https://arca.irht.cnrs.fr/ark:/63955/md67wm118346, CGM catalogue: https://ccfr.bnf.fr/portailccfr/jsp/index_view_direct_anonymous.jsp?record=eadcgm:EADC:D33A11487

Composite manuscript, 12-14th cent. 13th century for the musical items specified here. Origin: France, North. Provenance: Paris, St.-Victor abbey. Catalogue description: https://archivesetmanuscrits.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/cc73290g

In the previous and following folios there are other pieces notated with small square notation or even some late sort of French neumes on staves (15-16 cent.), particularly a prose for St. Victor (for the susceptio of his relics, 17 june): De profundis proclamemus et devote collaudemus... (f. 236). Composite manuscript. Provenance: Paris, St. Victor abbey. Catalogue description: https://archivesetmanuscrits.bnf.fr/ark:/12148/cc75874b

This piece follows to other Marian chants (Mira sunt prodigia and Virgo gaudet filio). The manuscript is a collection of "Opuscula theologica". Description in CGM: https://ccfr.bnf.fr/portailccfr/jsp/index_view_direct_anonymous.jsp?record=eadcgm:EADC:D08A11706 Notation: Diastematic neumes, West-Frankish, red ink four-line staves, clefs c, f, g and a.

library: Bibliothèque municipale (Bibliothèque d’Agglomération du Pays de Saint-Omer)
Bibliothèque municipale (Bibliothèque d’Agglomération du Pays de Saint-Omer)

notes: 13th century (the main manuscript). Provenance: Clairvaux (mentioned in a catalogue dated 1472 + ex-libris on f. 112v "Liber Sancte Marie Clarevallis". Catalogue CGM:  https://ccfr.bnf.fr/portailccfr/jsp/index_view_direct_anonymous.jsp?record=eadcgm:EADC:D02B10609 Online description with the digital reproduction: https://portail.mediatheque.grand-troyes.fr/iguana/www.main.cls?surl=search#RecordId=2.682 A fragment a four folios at the front (A, B) and back (C, D) of the manuscript contain musical pieces prepared to receive polyphonic notation but the notation was not made. CB131 is on the fol. Cv. This fragment The other conductus present in the fragment are: Ar Salvatoris hodie sanguis pregustatur, Av De nature fracto iure, 113r-v (=Cr-v) Flos de spina procreatur, 114r-v (=Dr-v) Hac in die Gedeonis

Catalogue: http://bilder.manuscripta-mediaevalia.de/hs//katalogseiten/HSK0495_b0698_jpg.htm Also here printed catalogue: https://dhb.thulb.uni-jena.de/rsc/viewer/ufb_derivate_00022997/CA-8-00032_0007.tif?logicalDiv=log_1ce749-e534-dbd6-f787be3b9 Composite manuscript. Origin at least for f. 88-89v: France (?) On f. 88r the Crusade song Chevalier mult estes guariz (picard scripta with k etc.). End of 12th cent. West Frankish neumes on staff, clef b/b square. On f. 89v-89r the addition of Axe Phebus is a later one, 13th cent. The neume notation is also different (still West Frankish neumes on staff, g and c clefs)

f. 72r: hic volucres referam sermone fideli Ista sunt nomina ferarum (CB 133).

Valentin Voith's Meistergesangbuch. See: Franzjosef Pensel: Verzeichnis der altdeutschen und ausgewählter neuerer deutscher Handschriften in der Universitätsbibliothek Jena, Berlin 1986 (Deutsche Texte des Mittelalters 70/2; Verzeichnisse altdeutscher Handschriften in der Deutschen Demokratischen Republik; 2), S. 279-307. Orationale Augensie. From Reichenau. Paper ms.

Part of the "Erlauer Marienklage" (german and latin songs). The ms. contains also a "Donaueschinger (sübairisches) Magdalenenspiel" (f. 7r and after). Origin: a region where a south Bavarian dialect was spoken. Date: ca. 1420-1425
1420-1435, Koblenz? Dominicans?

Cantionale, Geistliche Lieder mit Melodien. Münchner Marienklage. Contains latin monophonic and polyphonic pieces, also german sacred songs (marian songs). Provenance: Tegernsee, benedictine abbey. Date: third third of the 15th century. Rism catalogue description: https://opac.rism.info/rism/Record/rism456052828
1575, from Liesborn?

See Catalogue: https://opacplus.bsb-muenchen.de/title/BV037455752 Haimo von Auxerre: In apocalypsin. Date: 11th cent., 2nd quarter for the main part of the ms. The piece is an addition in the right margin of the first main folio. Difficult to give a datation but not prior to the late 12th century or early 13th since this is a poem by Ph. the Chancellor (1160?-1236). Origin: Tegernsee (benedictine abbey). Manuscript description (doesn't say a word about the musical addition): https://daten.digitale-sammlungen.de/0004/bsb00046212/images/index.html?id=00046212&groesser=&fip=193.174.98.30&no=&seite=123
https://api.digitale-sammlungen.de/iiif/presentation/v2/bsb00092129/manifest

Cantica canticorum cum glossis. Petrus Riga, Aurora. Date for the main parts of the ms: late 12th century or first half of the 13th. CB100 is copied as an addition, according to the printed catalogue in the 2nd half of the 13th cent. https://daten.digitale-sammlungen.de/~db/0010/bsb00106370/images/index.html Provenance: Benediktbeuren (former shelfmark Bened. 98).

Tractatus de arte musica. Carmina liturgica s. hymni. Date : c. 1400. Origin: Regensburg (cathedral?) Provenance: Augustiner-Chorherrenstift Diessen (arrived there in the 15th century). RISM description https://rism.online/sources/456052945 + Göllner 1993

"Hausbuch Michaels de Leone, 2nd vol. ("Würzburger Liederhandschrift"). Composite ms. with Latin and German texts. Origin: Würzburg. Date: mid-14th century (c. 1345-1354). Catalogue description: https://www.handschriftencensus.de/6441

Walther von der Vogelweide. Ms. description: https://collections.thulb.uni-jena.de/receive/HisBest_cbu_00008634 Origin: Mitteldeutschland/Niederdeutschland Date: 14th century, first quarter. Das zweispaltige Doppelblatt, das nicht das innerste Doppelblatt einer Lage bildet, überliefert neben 26 Walther-Strophen auch eine vollständige (Palästinalied) und drei fragmentarische Strophen-Melodien (zu Cormeau 115; zweiter Stollen des König-Friedrich-Tones; erster Stollen des 2. Philippstones) in gotischer deutscher Choralnotation und bildet damit die "wichtigste Quelle für Melodien Walthers" (Horst Brunner).

Cantionarium. Date: 13th century. Origin: Weingarten or (cf. Purcell-Joiner PhD 2017) Engelberg. Ms. description in print catalogue of the library: https://handschriftenportal.de/workspace?type=undefined&id=https%3A%2F%2Fiiif.ub.uni-leipzig.de%2F0000034986%2Fmanifest.json&page=183
Weingartner Liederhandschrift. Origin: Constance. Date: 1320s. Ms. description on Handschriftenportal: https://resolver.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/HSP0005FDD300000000
Nikolaus Irsch, Der Dom zu Trier (Die Kunstdenkmäler der Rheinprovinz 13, Die Kunstdenkmäler der Stadt Trier 1,1), Düsseldorf 1931.

Provenance: S. Simeon (entered the Treves library in 1802). Composite manuscript, the last part (f. 136-159) contains "gereimte lat. Gedichte", copied in the 15th century. library: Biblioteca della Curia generalizia dei Padri Domenicani di S. Sabina
notes: Manuscript held in the Archivio dei Dominicani di Santa Sabina (Roma). Dominican calendar from the same hand as the rest of the ms. at the begining, with the Feast of St. Thomas Aquinus added (canonized 1323). 14th century, before 1323? 16-leaf octonion quires. Thin parchment. Unknown origin. North of France? Chant book with hymn incipits, a processional, common and votive masses, a few offices (The Crown of Thorns, Peter the Martyr and the Translation of Saint Dominic) and a prosary. Added polyphonic pieces according to DIAMM, see the description: https://www.diamm.ac.uk/sources/846/#/

library: Monasterio de Santa María La Real de Las Huelgas
notes: Codex Las Huelgas

Origin: France? Bifolio with music pieces added to an incomplete copy of the Historia scholastica by Petrus Comestor, chancellor of Notre-Dame of Paris in the 1160s. Contains four monophonic conducti and one two-part motet, all belonging to the Notre-Dame repertoire. Most of the texts have medieval attribution to Philippe the Chancellor (d. 1236) in other sources. The manuscript does not seem to have originated in Spain. In fact, both handwritings do not display Spanish features and are rather closer to French scripts of the 13th century. See description in https://www.diamm.ac.uk/sources/3967/#/ Bevilacque:  "messine neumes". Note CC: ?? The axis of the notation and the shapes of the neumes are not at all lotharingian.

f-III-18 doesn't have this item on f. 179 (we have digital pictures of fol. 170-180). HS writes "Escorial, f.III.18, s. 14, fol. 179r. In Kurzzeilen geschr. Str. 2b fehlt". I've checked  f-III-18 as an old shelfmark, but the current shelfmark corresponding to the old f-III-18, d-III-3, doesn't have the piece either. I've browsed all the catalogues of the Escorial and couldn't find any match for the incipit Aristipe, for a fol. 179 with rhyme verses etc. I've checked the Greek ms. too. Couldn't find anything...
